title: andropogon nuttallii
intro: Andropogon nuttallii, also known as broom sedge, is a perennial grass native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Andropogon nuttallii include reducing pain and swelling, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
